name: penumbra-glspike-00-multi
description: Penumbra native-GLSL spike (Swath 4) regression: runs PenumbraGlMulti so the in-process GLSL ray-march conduit renders five atoms with distinct evaluators in one frame — four separately-compiled TAPES (sphere/box/torus/gyroid, concatenated into one tape texture with per-atom header offsets) + the Benchy mesh-SDF GRID. Proves the consolidated @penumbra/native compileSceneToGlArtifact path renders correctly in Rhino's GL context (shader compile + multi-tape dispatch + depth + progressive). The conduit can't be seen by CaptureToBitmap (it draws in PostDrawObjects), so on convergence it dumps the SDF render to a fixed PNG; this test file-source-captures that. Requires Penumbra.GlSpike.rhp registered for auto-load, and the scene.multi.json + benchy.grid.f32 artifacts present under packages/host-node/spike. v1 = capture mode (no baseline); approve the candidate, then switch to pixel-diff. NOTE: delete %LOCALAPPDATA%\Penumbra\GlSpike\active.png before the run (shared dump file — a failed converge would capture a stale frame); the conduit dumps once the frame settles to full quality (idle pump), so the checkpoint waits.

setup.vlmDescription: A Rhino viewport showing several distinct ray-marched SDF shapes in one row: a magenta sphere, a magenta rounded box, a magenta torus, a magenta gyroid lattice cube, and a blue toy boat (3DBenchy). Solid shaded surfaces, NOT an empty grey grid and NOT bounding boxes.
